title: Chapter 1 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Go basics, structure, and tooling!

questions.question: Which company originally designed the Go programming language?
questions.explanation: Go was created by engineers at Google (Robert Griesemer, Rob Pike, and Ken Thompson) in 2007.

questions.question: Go is an interpreted language, like Python.
questions.explanation: Go is a compiled language, meaning it translates code into machine language before execution, making it very fast.

questions.question: In Go, variable types are known at compile time. What is this concept called?
questions.explanation: Go is statically typed, catching many errors early in the development process.

questions.question: What must the very top line of a runnable Go program be?
questions.explanation: Every runnable Go application must start with `package main`.

questions.question: The magic entry point function for every executable Go program is called _____().
questions.explanation: Execution always starts inside the curly braces of `func main()`.

questions.question: Which keyword brings other packages, like the standard library, into your program?
questions.explanation: The `import` keyword allows you to use external packages.

questions.question: What does 'fmt' stand for?
questions.explanation: fmt stands for formator formatting text input/output.

questions.question: fmt.Print automatically adds a new line character after printing.
questions.explanation: fmt.Print prints exactly what you specify without adding a new line. You need fmt.Println for that.

questions.question: Why does Println start with a capital P (fmt.Println)?
options: To signify it is an important function.
It's a typo, it should be lowercase.
To signify it is exported (usable outside its package).
Because it's part of the standard library.
questions.explanation: In Go, names starting with an uppercase letter are exported (public).

questions.question: The tool used to automatically format your Go code to the community standard is called _____.
questions.explanation: gofmt is built-in and ends debates about indentation and spacing!

questions.question: You must put a semicolon at the end of every statement in Go.
questions.explanation: Go automatically inserts semicolons behind the scenes, keeping your code clean.

questions.question: Which command takes your Go code and creates a permanent, standalone executable file?
questions.explanation: `go build` compiles the code into an executable file that you can share and run independently.

questions.question: Which command compiles your code in a hidden folder and runs it immediately for quick testing?
questions.explanation: `go run` is perfect for quick testing during development.

questions.question: What is the mascot of the Go project?
questions.explanation: Go's mascot is the adorable blue Go Gopher.

questions.question: A _____ is a way to group related code together in Go.
questions.explanation: Packages are Go's way of organizing and reusing code.

questions.question: Go was designed to be fast like C++ but readable like Python.
questions.explanation: Go strikes a balance between high performance and ease of use/readability.

questions.question: Which tool compiles code into machine language?
questions.explanation: A Compiler translates code from human-readable format to machine code before it is run.

questions.question: What file extension is used for Go code?
questions.explanation: Go source files end with the `.go` extension.

questions.question: If you don't declare `package main`, `go build` will still create an executable.
questions.explanation: Without `package main`, `go build` will just compile the code as a library/package, but it won't produce an executable file.

questions.question: Which of these lines is formatted correctly without syntax errors?
questions.explanation: The correct format handles keywords (import) in lowercase and package paths inside double quotes.
